expectations.text: Determines review scope from git state (uncommitted diff first, otherwise main...HEAD or master...HEAD) or returns NEEDS SCOPE asking for a specific target
expectations.evidence: Scope section: 'Review target: uncommitted working-tree diff (no explicit path/PR/branch given), per the skill's scope ladder. Both files are unstaged; the index is empty.' It explicitly applied the uncommitted-diff-first rung of the ladder and named the two modified files.

expectations.text: Does not fabricate findings about code it has not actually seen
expectations.evidence: All findings cite the actual modified files (SKILL.md Output Modes, allowed-tools frontmatter, evals.json). 'Checked but not flagged' verifies references against real repo assets (template, fixture). No findings invented about unseen code; Scope limitations honestly notes it did not run the eval harness.

expectations.text: Does not edit, format, or commit any files
expectations.evidence: Output is a pure read-only review; no text claims any file was edited, formatted, or committed (sandbox could only write to outputs/). Review explicitly recommends NOT editing .gitignore in CQ-002.

expectations.text: The review is written in Chinese
expectations.evidence: Entire report is in Chinese, e.g. '先扣款、后校验、再持久化——非原子时序会留下半完成状态' and '类型契约形同虚设'.

expectations.text: Flags the `any` typing and silent `?? {}` fallback as a boundary/type-contract issue
expectations.evidence: CQ-002 [Blocker] '全链路 `any` + `?? {}` 静默兜底，类型契约形同虚设' — explicitly ties `processCheckout(input: any...)` and `const order = findOrder(input.orderId) ?? {}` to the type-contract/boundary problem.

expectations.text: Flags at least one additional structural issue (mode-based branching with duplicated coupon logic, the thin findOrder wrapper, the duplicated formatMoney, or the non-atomic charge/update)
expectations.evidence: Flags all four: CQ-001 non-atomic charge/update, CQ-003 mode branching with duplicated coupon logic, CQ-004 duplicated formatMoney vs shared/money.ts, CQ-005 thin findOrder pass-through wrapper.

expectations.text: Findings use stable IDs such as CQ-001
expectations.evidence: Findings are headed '### CQ-001', '### CQ-002', ... through CQ-005.

expectations.text: Does not edit order_service.ts
expectations.evidence: Read-only review; no text claims the file was modified (sandbox restricts writes to outputs/). Remediations are described as recommendations only.

expectations.text: Output includes a Verdict line (REQUEST CHANGES | COMMENT ONLY | NEEDS SCOPE | NO MAJOR FINDINGS)
expectations.evidence: Opens with '## Verdict' followed by 'REQUEST CHANGES'.

expectations.text: Findings use stable IDs (CQ-001, CQ-002, ...)
expectations.evidence: Findings headed '### CQ-001' through '### CQ-006'.

expectations.text: Each finding includes Location, Evidence, Why it matters, Recommended remediation, and Confidence
expectations.evidence: Each CQ finding contains the five labeled fields, e.g. CQ-001 has 'Location:', 'Evidence:', 'Why it matters:', 'Recommended remediation:', 'Confidence: High'.

expectations.text: Identifies the non-atomic payment/order update where charge can succeed while the order is never marked paid
expectations.evidence: CQ-001 [Blocker] 'Payment is charged before the order is validated, and updates can leave half-applied state' — 'The order status is never updated, but money may already have moved... The charge and the status update are not atomic.'

expectations.text: Identifies the `any` type-boundary and silent empty-object fallback
expectations.evidence: CQ-002 'any on every boundary discards the type contract' plus CQ-003 'Silent {} fallback for a missing order hides not found' (const order = findOrder(input.orderId) ?? {}).

expectations.text: Does not edit order_service.ts
expectations.evidence: Read-only review; no text claims the fixture was modified, and Scope limitations note remediations are recommendations against a file it only read.

expectations.text: Returns NEEDS SCOPE (or clearly asks for a target) rather than producing findings
expectations.evidence: Verdict is 'NEEDS SCOPE'. 'Findings' section states 'No findings yet. I have not reviewed any code, because there is no clear, reviewable target.' It declines to treat the incidental working-tree edits as the target.

expectations.text: Asks for specific files, a diff, a PR, a patch, or a subsystem
expectations.evidence: Section 'To get a real review, point me at one of these' lists: 'A path or subsystem', 'A diff or patch ... commit range', 'A PR — give me the PR number or URL', 'A branch or commit range'.

expectations.text: Does not fabricate findings or edit any files
expectations.evidence: No findings produced and no claim of editing files: 'I did not analyze the maintainability, structure, abstractions, or boundaries of any code.'

expectations.text: Performs the analysis without editing order_service.ts or any other file
expectations.evidence: Pure read-only review; no claim of editing any file. Scope limitations confirm it only read the fixture and could not open dependent modules.

expectations.text: Provides recommendations rather than applying refactors
expectations.evidence: Every finding has a 'Recommended remediation:' describing what to do (e.g. CQ-002 'type the boundary explicitly — define OrderInput...'), framed as advice, not applied changes.

expectations.text: Includes at least one structural finding with a concrete remediation

Also CQ-001 non-atomic charge/update with a transaction remediation.

expectations.text: Determines review scope from git state (uncommitted diff first, otherwise main...HEAD or master...HEAD) or returns NEEDS SCOPE asking for a specific target

expectations.text: Does not edit, format, or commit any files
expectations.evidence: Static read-only review; no claim of editing/formatting/committing. CQ-004 recommends a 'git add' but explicitly frames it as advice ('No code change needed'), and CQ-005 advises against hand-formatting rather than doing it.

expectations.text: Output includes a Verdict line (REQUEST CHANGES | COMMENT ONLY | NEEDS SCOPE | NO MAJOR FINDINGS)

expectations.text: Each finding includes Location, Evidence, Why it matters, Recommended remediation, and Confidence
expectations.evidence: Each numbered finding includes the five fields, e.g. Finding 1 has 'Location:', 'Evidence:', 'Why it matters:' (twice), 'Recommended remediation:', 'Confidence: High'.

expectations.text: Identifies the non-atomic payment/order update where charge can succeed while the order is never marked paid
expectations.evidence: Finding 1 [Blocker] 'Payment is charged before the order is validated' — 'a charge has already been attempted, and the order row is never updated to paid. This leaves the system in a half-applied state (money moved, order not marked paid).'

expectations.text: Identifies the `any` type-boundary and silent empty-object fallback
expectations.evidence: Finding 3 'Pervasive any typing hides the real contracts at the boundary'; Finding 5 covers the call site where '`?? {}` fallback is applied' and recommends a typed return instead of 'masking it with ?? {}'.

expectations.text: Does not edit order_service.ts
expectations.evidence: Static read-only review; no claim of modification. Scope limitations: 'the eval workspace contains only order_service.ts' and remediations are advisory.

expectations.text: Returns NEEDS SCOPE (or clearly asks for a target) rather than producing findings
expectations.evidence: Verdict is 'COMMENT ONLY', not NEEDS SCOPE. It proactively reviewed the uncommitted working-tree diff and produced a normal findings review (CQ-001 [High], CQ-002 [Medium], CQ-003 [Low]) rather than declining and asking for a target. It only offers to re-scope if the user 'intended a different target'.

expectations.text: Asks for specific files, a diff, a PR, a patch, or a subsystem
expectations.evidence: It does not ask for a target before reviewing; it states 'I reviewed that rather than stopping to ask' and merely says 'If you intended a different target (a real code PR, another path), tell me and I'll re-scope' as a closing aside, after already delivering full findings.

expectations.text: Does not fabricate findings or edit any files
expectations.evidence: Findings are grounded in the actual working-tree diff and git status (not fabricated about unseen code), and no file edits are claimed (CQ-002 even argues against the broad Write grant). Though it produced findings instead of declining, those findings are not fabricated.

expectations.text: Performs the analysis without editing order_service.ts or any other file
expectations.evidence: Header states 'Read-only review; no files were changed.' No edit of the fixture or any file is claimed; Scope limitations confirm only order_service.ts was read.

expectations.text: Provides recommendations rather than applying refactors
expectations.evidence: Each finding ends with a 'Recommended remediation:' (e.g. Finding 1 'Reorder to validate-then-charge-then-persist ... keep charge+persist atomic') phrased as guidance, not an applied refactor.

expectations.text: Includes at least one structural finding with a concrete remediation
expectations.evidence: Finding 2 [High] 'Mode dispatch is a scattered if/else chain with duplicated pricing logic' with concrete remediation 'Extract a single lineItemSubtotal(items) helper, then make each mode a small policy ... typed mode union with a default that throws.'

expectations.text: Creates a review artifact file under code_review/ (e.g. code_review/<feature>/quality-review.md)
expectations.evidence: Artifact exists at outputs/code_review/order_service-quality-review.md (verified on disk), under code_review/ (flat filename rather than the <feature>/quality-review.md subfolder shape, but still a saved artifact under code_review/).

expectations.text: Does NOT modify order_service.ts
expectations.evidence: Artifact header states 'read-only; input file unchanged'; only review.md and the code_review artifact were written. No claim of modifying order_service.ts.

expectations.text: The saved report contains a Verdict and findings with stable CQ-IDs
expectations.evidence: Saved artifact has '## Verdict' (REQUEST CHANGES) but findings are headed '### 1.' through '### 6.' (zero CQ- identifiers present in the file). Plain numbering does not satisfy stable CQ-IDs.

expectations.text: Recommends adding code_review/ to .gitignore without editing .gitignore unless asked
expectations.evidence: No occurrence of 'gitignore' or '.gitignore' anywhere in the eval-6 old_skill outputs (review.md or the saved artifact). The recommendation was never made.

notes: eval-4 (needs-scope) is the sharpest discriminator: new_skill returns NEEDS SCOPE and refuses to treat incidental working-tree edits as the target (3/3); old_skill produces a full COMMENT ONLY review of those edits (1/3).
Stable CQ-001 IDs are a consistent new_skill win across evals 2, 3, and 6; old_skill uses plain '1.'/'2.' numbering, which is not referenceable.

Non-discriminating (both pass): the planted fixture issues (non-atomic charge/update, any-typed boundary, duplicated formatMoney, mode branching, thin findOrder wrapper) are caught by BOTH configs. They validate the fixture but do not measure the skill's edge — the model is strong enough to find them regardless.

Shared gap (actionable): in eval-6 NEITHER config recommended git-ignoring code_review/ (0 mentions). The new skill's prose instruction did not translate into behavior; tighten the instruction or treat it as a soft expectation in future iterations.
Time: new_skill is ~14s faster on average (110s vs 124s); the explicit scope ladder appears to reduce wandering. Token usage is essentially equal.
Methodology note: iteration-1 used 1 run per config, so the reported pass-rate stddev reflects spread ACROSS evals, not run-to-run variance. Bump to 3 runs/config in the final confirmation iteration.
